governance_posts.title: [TEMP CHECK] Deploy a Dedicated Aave V4 Whitelabel Instance fully managed by EtherFi on OP Mainnet to Power Ether.fi Cash
governance_posts.description:
Author: Ether.Fi

Date: July 1, 2026

Summary

EtherFi requests that a dedicated, EtherFi operated Aave V4 hub on OP Mainnet is deployed to serve as the credit backend for EtherFi Cash, our Visa card product used by tens of thousands of cardholders. This instance would replace the bespoke borrow/lend market (“Debt Manager”) that powers Cash today.

The instance is isolated and whitelisted: EtherFi operates it end to end - configuration, risk parameters, liquidity, and growth, while Aave provides the V4 deployment and operating license and earns a share of the revenue it generates. Because the instance is ring-fenced and EtherFi run, Aave carries the upside without taking on the market’s day-to-day risk.

The proposal arrives with a substantial commercial package contributed by EtherFi and the Optimism Foundation, summarized in the Commercial Terms section, including a 20% revenue share to the Aave DAO, GHO integration into EtherFi Cash, an Aave-deployed GHO GSM on OP Mainnet, full migration of the EtherFi Debt Manager, up to $175M in assets at launch, and product exclusivity to Aave V4.

Motivation

EtherFi Cash today. Cash lets cardholders spend against yield-bearing collateral at the point of sale: they borrow a stablecoin to settle a Visa transaction while preserving their underlying asset exposure. It runs in production on OP Mainnet on a custom, non-pooled borrow/lend market with roughly $25M in active borrows across 16+ collateral assets and a high-frequency, low-ticket, well-distributed borrow profile. We are targeting roughly $500M in assets on the instance by the end of 2026.

Why migrate to Aave V4. Maintaining a bespoke lending engine is increasingly operationally taxing. Migrating to a dedicated Aave V4 instance lets EtherFi inherit audited, battle-tested infrastructure and governance machinery while preserving the Cash product surface (User Safes, Credit/Debit modes, settlement) above it.

Why this is a fit for Aave.

  • Pure-upside revenue. Aave shares in the borrow revenue of a live consumer product without committing pool liquidity or balance sheet to it. At our end-2026 target scale, the instance is projected to generate an estimated $5-6M in annual revenue, 20% of which accrues directly to the Aave DAO and compounds as the Cash book grows.

  • Direct GHO adoption. GHO becomes a supply/borrow reserve on the instance as soon as GHO is deployed on Optimism, with an Aave-deployed GSM strengthening GHO’s peg and liquidity on OP Mainnet.

  • GHO as a spend currency. Beyond being a listed reserve, GHO could, at the Aave DAO discretion, be enabled as a deposit/spend asset in Cash, subject to sufficient GHO liquidity to support the Cash program as expected, turning card volume into organic GHO demand.

  • Real-world spend footprint. Aave extends into consumer card settlement, anchored by a product with tens of thousands of active cardholders.

  • Net new on-chain activity on OP Mainnet, with exclusivity to Aave V4 for the Cash product.

Aave Labs has expressed support for deploying the instance and providing the operating license. This Temp Check initiates the governance process to ratify that direction and to confirm community sentiment ahead of an ARFC.

Specification

This Temp Check is intentionally high-level; detailed parameters and the technical specification will be finalized at the ARFC stage with service-provider input.

Architecture. A dedicated Aave V4 hub on OP Mainnet. One of V4’s first Layer 2 targets, with spokes depending on the final lending architecture. The instance is whitelisted and isolated from Aave’s shared liquidity and other markets. EtherFi operates the instance end to end and is responsible for collateral listing, risk parameters, oracles, and ongoing risk management. Aave provides the V4 codebase under license for 2 years initial term, but is not responsible for its assets, configuration, or risk.

Operating model. EtherFi acts as operator and will authorize an independent risk admin for the instance, with authority over:

  • Collateral listing and de-listing

  • Oracle selection (Chainlink, RedStone, or otherwise) and configuration

  • LTV, liquidation threshold, and liquidation bonus per asset

  • Interest rate models, supporting both utilization-curve and fixed-rate reserves

  • Supply and borrow caps, and pause states

EtherFi and the independent risk admin will handle, as appropriate, items such as configuration, liquidity sourcing, and market growth in full.

Aave’s role. Aave Labs supports EtherFi in the deployment of the V4 instance on OP Mainnet and provides the license to run the whitelisted version, and deploys a GHO GSM on OP Mainnet to support GHO stability and peg.

Collateral at launch. Mirror the existing Cash collateral set, with streamlined additions over time via the admin role:

  • ETH-likes: weETH, wETH

  • BTC-likes: eBTC

  • Stables: USDC, USDT, EURC, frxUSD, GHO

  • EtherFi platform, Optimism & HYPE: ETHFI / sETHFI, eUSD, OP, beHYPE, wHYPE

  • Liquid vault receipts: LiquidETH, LiquidBTC, LiquidUSD, LiquidReserve

Borrow reserves. USDC and GHO, added as both a supply and a borrowable asset.

Liquidations. Aave V4’s standard partial liquidation behavior; EtherFi adapts its liquidator tooling to the V4 flow.

Commercial Terms

The following package has been aligned in principle between EtherFi, the Optimism Foundation, and Aave Labs to strengthen the instance at launch. Final mechanics are subject to this governance process and service-provider review.

  • Revenue share to Aave DAO: 80–20 EtherFi / Aave on instance reserve-factor revenue. 20% to the Aave treasury, settled automatically. This split reflects the long-standing, proven relationship between EtherFi and Aave, together with Aave V4 serving as the sole lending and borrowing market for EtherFi Cash.

  • GHO integration: GHO supported on the instance as both a supply and a borrowable reserve once GHO is deployed on OP Mainnet. Aave deploys a GHO GSM on Optimism.

  • GHO as a spend currency: at the Aave DAO’s discretion and conditional on sufficient GHO liquidity for the Cash program, GHO may also be added as a spendable/deposit asset within Cash itself, converting a portion of card usage into direct GHO demand.

  • Assets at launch: EtherFi brings up to $175M in assets to the instance at launch, with a clear path to grow significantly from there.

  • Exclusivity: EtherFi Cash will exclusively use Aave V4 lending markets as DeFi Lending Protocol.

  • Launch capitalization (EtherFi & Optimism Foundation funded):

    • $20M supplied from the Optimism Foundation Treasury into the instance.

    • $1.2M joint incentive package, directed toward deposits and/or borrows across any asset.

    • $5M strategic GHO position, taken via the GHO GSM, shared by the Optimism Foundation and EtherFi, held for 6 months, after which continuation is at each party’s discretion.

Taken together, Aave contributes the deployment, license, and GHO infrastructure; EtherFi and the Optimism Foundation contribute the launch capital, incentives, asset base and ongoing operation, with revenue flowing back to the Aave DAO.

Disclaimer

This proposal is submitted by EtherFi as the operator of EtherFi Cash and the proposer of the instance. The commercial terms reflect agreements in principle with Aave Labs and Optimism Foundation; all terms and parameters are subject to this governance process, ARFC refinement, and service-provider review. EtherFi is not compensated by any third party for submitting this proposal.

Next Steps

  1. Gather community feedback for a minimum of 5 days.

  2. Escalate to a TEMP CHECK Snapshot vote.

  3. On a successful Snapshot, proceed to an ARFC with finalized parameters and risk/finance service-provider input, targeting a July 2026 deployment to meet the Cash product handoff from the current Debt Manager.

governance_posts.title: Risk Stewards: Change of Supply and Borrow Caps on Aave V3 / 2026.07.05
governance_posts.description:
Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following supply and borrow cap changes on the Aave V3 MegaETH and Monad instances. The changes fall into two groups: cap increases on two reserves that are pinned at 100% of their supply cap, and a recalibration of the Monad reserves whose launch caps sit above realized usage and available liquidity. As detailed below, the two saturated reserves are consumed by a small number of leveraged looping positions operating close to the liquidation threshold, so the increases are supported by demonstrated demand but carry concentrated liquidation risk that we flag for monitoring.

Aave V3 MegaETH:

  • Increase stcUSD supply cap from 10,000,000 to 20,000,000.

Aave V3 Monad:

  • Increase syrupUSDC supply cap from 40,000,000 to 80,000,000.
  • Reduce sUSDe supply cap from 60,000,000 to 20,000,000.
  • Reduce USDe supply cap from 60,000,000 to 20,000,000 and borrow cap from 50,000,000 to 18,000,000.
  • Reduce cbBTC supply cap from 1,000 to 400.
  • Reduce WETH supply cap from 40,000 to 14,100 and borrow cap from 36,000 to 12,400.
  • Reduce wstETH supply cap from 35,000 to 4,600.
  • Reduce weETH supply cap from 30,000 to 10,300.

Cap Increases

stcUSD (MegaETH)

stcUSD supply stands at the 10,000,000 supply cap, with no remaining headroom for new deposits. The reserve has absorbed its full cap and continued demand cannot be accommodated at the current setting. We propose doubling the supply cap to 20,000,000.

The composition of that supply is concentrated. Two wallets account for essentially the entire cap (approximately 5.14M and 4.85M stcUSD, roughly $5.5M and $5.2M respectively), and both run the same leveraged strategy: supply stcUSD as collateral and borrow USDm against it. Each sits at a health factor of about 1.03. The remaining suppliers are dust. In other words, the 100% cap utilization reflects two thin-margin looping positions rather than broad organic demand, so doubling the cap primarily creates room for those actors (or the same strategy replicated) to extend leverage. The increase is reasonable given the liqudity situation and initial adoption, but the concentration and the health-factor cluster near 1.0 warrant continued monitoring of liquidity for USDm and stcUSD to ensure liquidations of these positions can be absorbed.


Source: LlamaRisk, July 5, 2026

syrupUSDC (Monad)

syrupUSDC supply on Monad is at the 40,000,000 supply cap. As with stcUSD, the reserve is saturated and cannot take additional deposits. We propose doubling the supply cap to 80,000,000 to accommodate continued demand.

The supply is highly concentrated and, as on MegaETH, is driven by leveraged looping. A single wallet supplies approximately 26M syrupUSDC (about $30.9M, or roughly 65% of the entire cap), borrowing about $27.8M of USDT0 and USDC against it at a health factor of about 1.02. The next fifteen suppliers run the identical loop, each at a health factor between roughly 1.02 and 1.09. As with stcUSD, the cap is consumed by a small number of positions operating just above the liquidation threshold. Before proceeding with further increases, we will continue to monitor the borrow-side stablecoin liquidity on Monad that these positions would need to unwind against.


Source: LlamaRisk, July 5, 2026

Monad Recalibration

The remaining Monad reserves carry launch-era caps that were sized generously relative to the DEX liquidity and usage the market has developed since deployment. Realized utilization on each of these reserves is in the low single digits as a share of the current supply cap. Right-sizing the caps toward observed demand reduces the protocol’s tail exposure to reserves whose on-chain and DEX liquidity does not yet support the deployed caps, while leaving some headroom above current balances.

sUSDe (Monad)

Current supply is approximately 2.65M sUSDe (about $3.28M) against the 60,000,000 supply cap, or roughly 4.4% utilization. We propose reducing the supply cap to 20,000,000, which remains an order of magnitude above the current balance and aligns the ceiling with realized demand. This would be re-evaluated after proper DEX liquidity is formed.

USDe (Monad)

USDe supply is negligible (approximately 100 tokens) against the 60,000,000 supply cap, and there is no outstanding borrow against the 50,000,000 borrow cap. We propose reducing the supply cap to 20,000,000 and the borrow cap to 18,000,000. This would be re-evaluated after proper DEX liquidity is formed.

cbBTC (Monad)

Current supply is approximately 3.5 cbBTC (about $0.22M) against the 1,000 supply cap, or roughly 0.3% utilization. Based on current liquidity conditions, we target a $25M notional ceiling which equates to 400 cbBTC. This preserves substantial headroom above current usage while aligning the cap with the reserve’s liquidity profile.

WETH (Monad)

Current supply is approximately 3,217 WETH (about $5.70M, 8.0% of cap) with approximately 1,982 WETH borrowed (5.5% of the borrow cap). Based on current liquidity conditions, we target a $25M supply notional, which equates to 14,100 WETH. Borrow cap is accordingly set to 12,400 WETH. Both new caps remain well above current balances.

wstETH (Monad)

Current supply is approximately 1,100 wstETH (about $2.41M) against the 35,000 supply cap, or roughly 3.1% utilization. Targeting a $10M notional ceiling equates to 4,600 wstETH. This would be re-evaluated after proper DEX liquidity has formed.

weETH (Monad)

Current supply is approximately 673 weETH (about $1.31M) against the 30,000 supply cap, or roughly 2.2% utilization. Targeting a $20M notional ceiling equates to 10,300 weETH. While DEX liquidity continues to increase for this asset, further caps raises would be contingent upon the capacity of WETH reserve.

Specification

Instance Asset Parameter Current Proposed Approx. USD
Aave V3 MegaETH stcUSD supplyCap 10,000,000 20,000,000 ~$21.4M
Aave V3 Monad syrupUSDC supplyCap 40,000,000 80,000,000 ~$93.7M
Aave V3 Monad sUSDe supplyCap 60,000,000 20,000,000 ~$24.7M
Aave V3 Monad USDe supplyCap 60,000,000 20,000,000 ~$20.0M
Aave V3 Monad USDe borrowCap 50,000,000 18,000,000 ~$18.0M
Aave V3 Monad cbBTC supplyCap 1,000 400 ~$25.1M
Aave V3 Monad WETH supplyCap 40,000 14,100 ~$25.0M
Aave V3 Monad WETH borrowCap 36,000 12,400 ~$22.0M
Aave V3 Monad wstETH supplyCap 35,000 4,600 ~$10.1M
Aave V3 Monad weETH supplyCap 30,000 10,300 ~$20.0M

Next Steps

We will move forward and implement these updates via the Risk Steward process.

Disclosure

This review was independently prepared by LlamaRisk, a DeFi risk service provider funded in part by the Aave DAO. LlamaRisk is not directly affiliated with the protocol(s) reviewed in this assessment and did not receive any compensation from the protocol(s) or their affiliated entities for this work.

The information provided should not be construed as legal, financial, tax, or professional advice.

governance_posts.title: Risk Stewards: Supply and Borrow Cap Reductions on Aave V3 / 2026.07.01
governance_posts.description:
Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following parameter changes based on user behavior, on-chain liquidity, and position health observed in the latest review of Aave V3 reserves.

Aave V3 Linea:

  • Reduce supply cap for WETH from 7,950 to 6,250.
  • Reduce borrow cap for WETH from 7,150 to 2,370.
  • Reduce supply cap for weETH from 5,000 to 2,880.
  • Reduce supply cap for USDC from 20,000,000 to 2,320,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for USDC from 18,000,000 to 1,180,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for USDT from 20,000,000 to 708,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for USDT from 18,000,000 to 378,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for mUSD from 500,000 to 55,700.
  • Reduce borrow cap for mUSD from 450,000 to 18,200.
  • Reduce supply cap for WBTC from 40 to 14.
  • Reduce supply cap for wstETH from 500 to 267.

Cap Reductions

governance_posts.title: Introducing Maxshot AI as a Strategy Operator in the Aave Ecosystem
governance_posts.description:
1. Summary

This proposal introduces Maxshot AI to the Aave community as a complementary, value-accretive yield optimization and distribution layer.

As Maxshot is already integrated with Aave V3 markets across multiple chains, this proposal does not request any protocol parameter changes, risk parameter updates, or direct integrations. Instead, it seeks community awareness, feedback, and formal ecosystem recognition of Maxshot as a third-party strategy operator that enhances capital efficiency and user experience for Aave.

By leveraging AI-driven, non-discretionary agents, Maxshot simplifies access to optimized, omni-chain yield opportunities, with the goal of increasing total value locked (TVL), capital efficiency, and liquidity stickiness across Aave markets.

2. Abstract

Maxshot AI operates as an intelligent yield optimization layer built on top of Aave and other DeFi protocols. It automatically routes and rebalances capital across chains and markets using deterministic, AI-driven agents to maximize risk-adjusted returns.

This proposal aims to align the Aave community with an existing ecosystem partner that already contributes capital flows into Aave liquidity pools and improves user access to yield opportunities without introducing additional protocol risk.

3. Motivation

Aave is a foundational DeFi liquidity protocol with robust risk management, transparent governance, and deployments across multiple networks. While its multi-chain expansion is a core strength, it also introduces structural inefficiencies that create opportunities for complementary, user-layer optimization.

Key Challenges

  • Liquidity Fragmentation: Liquidity is distributed across multiple Aave deployments, requiring users to manually bridge assets, compare rates, and manage positions across chains.

  • Operational Complexity: Actively managing positions across multiple markets and networks is time-intensive and costly, leading to suboptimal capital allocation.

  • Uneven Utilization: Friction at the user layer can result in idle liquidity and inconsistent utilization across Aave markets.

Maxshot’s Role

Maxshot AI acts as a non-custodial, intelligent routing and optimization layer on top of Aave, improving capital efficiency and user experience through automated, rules-based yield optimization—without modifying or competing with Aave’s core protocol.

4. Specification: Maxshot Overview

Maxshot AI is an AI Agent Factory, an AI-native, automation-first infrastructure layer designed to build, deploy, and coordinate autonomous agents for on-chain capital optimization.

At its core, Maxshot provides a standardized framework for creating protocol-aware, risk-constrained AI agents that can observe market conditions, reason over strategy sets, and execute actions across DeFi systems. These agents operate continuously within predefined risk models and governance-aligned parameters, enabling scalable and transparent automation at the infrastructure level.

Key Characteristics

  • Deterministic, Continuous Execution

    Agents operate autonomously and continuously, responding to market changes without manual intervention. All decisions follow transparent, rules-based logic that is auditable and verifiable on-chain, eliminating discretionary risk.

  • Full On-Chain Transparency

    Every allocation, rebalance, and performance metric is recorded on-chain and available for real-time community review, enabling ongoing verification of compliance and protocol impact.

  • Separation of Concerns

    Maxshot enforces a strict separation between optimization logic (Strategy Layer), safety constraints (Risk Layer), and on-chain interactions (Execution Layer). This design ensures that strategy decisions cannot bypass risk controls and that all actions remain auditable.

The first flagship product built on this framework is Maxshot Omni Yield Aggregator, an omni-chain, multi-protocol yield optimization system live across multiple blockchains. Powered by a coordinated network of autonomous agents, the aggregator dynamically allocates capital in real time rather than relying on static strategies or manual rebalancing.

As the agent factory expands, Maxshot is designed to support new on-chain use cases beyond yield optimization, while maintaining strict alignment with protocol risk frameworks and ecosystem incentives.

5. Technical Architecture

Maxshot follows a protocol-agnostic, modular architecture optimized for transparency, security, and auditability. The system consists of three distinct layers:

Strategy Layer

Defines optimization logic and allocation objectives by analyzing real-time market data across Aave and other protocols. It generates allocation recommendations but has no execution authority.

Risk Layer

Acts as the system’s safety guardrail, enforcing predefined constraints such as protocol, asset, and chain exposure limits. Any action that violates these rules is automatically blocked and logged for transparency.

Execution Layer

Handles all on-chain operations, including deposits, withdrawals, and rebalancing. Execution is deterministic and fully auditable, and only proceeds after Risk Layer approval.

This three-layer architecture ensures that strategy optimization, risk management, and execution transparency are never compromised. Each layer operates independently, and no single layer has authority over the entire system. This design reduces the risk of catastrophic failures and ensures that the Aave community retains full visibility and control over Maxshot’s operations.

6. Rationale & Differentiation

Maxshot AI is an AI-native, automation-native financial infrastructure designed to manage and optimize on-chain financial activities.

Unlike traditional yield aggregators that rely on static strategies or manual rebalancing, Maxshot operates as a network of non-discretionary, autonomous agents that continuously evaluate market conditions and execute allocation decisions in real time, strictly within predefined risk constraints and governance-aligned parameters.

Core Advantages

  1. Omni-chain, Multi-protocol Optimization

    Capital is optimized globally across chains and protocols rather than siloed within a single deployment.

  2. Agent-driven (Non-discretionary) Execution

    Deterministic agents execute strategies continuously without human intervention or discretionary decision-making.

  3. Separation of Strategy, Risk, and Execution

    A modular architecture enforces clear boundaries between optimization logic, risk controls, and execution, enhancing security, transparency, and independent auditability.

This architecture allows Maxshot to function not merely as a yield aggregator, but as a general-purpose AI agent platform, where additional agents (such as risk agents, liquidity routing agents, governance-aware execution agents, and protocol-specific strategy agents) can be composed and deployed over time.

7. Live Deployments & Metrics

Maxshot has been live in production since December 2025, and has already attracted meaningful TVL, demonstrating operational readiness and early market validation.

The platform currently manages approximately $9.8M in total TVL across live deployments, including ~$1.8M in the Omni USDC vault. All deployments are fully on-chain and publicly verifiable, reducing the governance and execution risk typically associated with new strategy operators.

Strategy Asset Supported Chains TVL Status
Maxshot Omni USDC USDC Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism $1.82M Live
Maxshot Omni USDT0 USDT - - Launching this month

Real-time performance and allocation data are available on the Maxshot dashboard:

https://app.maxshot.ai/#

8. Performance Observations

Maxshot omni-chain vaults prioritize stable, risk-adjusted returns rather than short-term yield maximization.

The Maxshot Omni USDC vault has historically delivered APYs ranging between approximately 2% and 10% over the past month, with a recent 24-hour APY of 7.76%. Performance data is fully transparent and available in real time via the Maxshot application.

Period APY Volatility Max Drawdown
1 Day 7.76% Low 0.00%
7 Day (Est.) 6.82% Low 0.15%
14 Day (Est.) 6.25% Low 0.25%
21 Day (Est.) 6.10% Low 0.35%
30 Day (Est.) 5.07% Low 0.50%

Key Observation:

Maxshot delivers stable, risk-adjusted returns rather than volatile peak yields. The ~5.07% 30-day average APY reflects a sustainable return after accounting for market volatility and rebalancing costs, while low drawdowns indicate effective risk management and controlled concentration.

9. Operational Readiness

Maxshot demonstrates strong operational and compliance readiness through the following factors:

  • Track Record

Maxshot has operated continuously since December 2025, managing real capital across multiple chains and protocols without incidents or user fund losses. The platform has rebalanced through changing market conditions while maintaining stable performance.

  • TVL Experience

With approximately $9.8M in total TVL across live deployments (including ~$1.8M in the Omni USDC vault), Maxshot has demonstrated the ability to manage meaningful capital and validate its infrastructure and risk controls.

  • Technical Capability

Maxshot is built on proven DeFi architecture and continuous monitoring systems, and employs industry-standard security practices including multi-signature controls, timelocks, emergency and pause mechanisms.

  • Operational Duration

Maxshot has been live in production for over one month, providing sufficient operational history to demonstrate system stability, performance consistency, and effective risk management.

10. User & Ecosystem Benefits

For Users

  • One-click access to optimized, omni-chain yield

    Users deposit into a single vault and gain exposure to the best available risk-adjusted yields across supported markets and supported chains, without manually bridging or managing multiple positions.

  • Automated capital rebalancing with reduced operational overhead

    Autonomous agents continuously monitor market conditions and rebalance capital in real time, saving users time, gas costs, and the need for active position management.

  • Transparent, real-time performance visibility

    All allocations, rebalances, and returns are recorded on-chain, allowing users to independently verify performance, fees, and capital deployment at any time.

For DAOs & Institutions

  • Custom or branded strategies with tailored risk profiles

    DAOs and institutions can launch protocol- or treasury-specific strategies with defined risk parameters, allocation constraints, and optimization objectives aligned with their mandates.

  • Treasury-grade reporting and on-chain transparency

    Detailed analytics, allocation breakdowns, and historical transaction records support institutional treasury management, auditability, and compliance requirements.

For the Aave Protocol

  • Increased TVL and user growth

    Lower access friction and automated yield optimization attract new users and capital into Aave markets across multiple networks.

  • Improved capital efficiency across markets

    Dynamic allocation routes liquidity to the most productive pools, reducing idle capital and improving overall utilization rates.

  • Enhanced liquidity stickiness

    Consistent, optimized returns encourage users to retain capital within the ecosystem rather than migrating to competing protocols.

This proposal requests the following from the Aave community:

  • Community review and feedback on Maxshot’s proposed role, design, and alignment within the Aave ecosystem, including any concerns or recommendations from contributors and risk stakeholders.

  • Recognition of Maxshot AI as a complementary, third-party strategy operator, operating on top of Aave without modifying core protocol parameters or competing with native Aave products.

  • Exploration of optional future collaboration opportunities, such as co-marketing initiatives, ecosystem showcases, or joint educational efforts, subject to community interest and further discussion.

11. Formal Requests

Maxshot requests the following from the Aave community:

  • Community review and feedback on Maxshot’s proposed role, design, and alignment within the Aave ecosystem, including any concerns or recommendations from contributors and risk stakeholders.

  • Recognition of Maxshot AI as a complementary, third-party strategy operator, operating on top of Aave without modifying core protocol parameters or competing with native Aave products.

  • Exploration of optional future collaboration opportunities, such as co-marketing initiatives, ecosystem showcases, or joint educational efforts, subject to community interest and further discussion.

12. Risk Assessment

This proposal introduces no direct protocol risk, as:

  • No changes to Aave smart contracts or protocol parameters are requested or required

  • Aave governance retains full control over all protocol operations, risk settings, and upgrade decisions

  • Maxshot interacts with Aave solely as a standard external user, using existing, permissionless interfaces

Any risks specific to Maxshot are isolated from the Aave protocol and are mitigated through a modular system architecture, deterministic and transparent on-chain execution, continuous monitoring and automated safeguards, and the operational experience of the Maxshot team managing live, multi-chain deployments.

13. Community Discussion Topics

We welcome feedback from the Aave community on the following points:

  • The role of AI-driven yield optimizers within the Aave ecosystem, including how automated strategy layers can complement Aave’s core markets while respecting protocol risk frameworks.

  • Desired benchmarks, safeguards, or constraints for recognized strategy operators, such as transparency standards, risk limits, reporting expectations, or operational maturity.

  • Best practices for collaboration between Aave and ecosystem partners, including governance communication, risk disclosures, and ongoing monitoring.

  • Preferences for future integrations or co-branded initiatives, such as ecosystem showcases, educational content, or user-facing integrations that align with Aave’s long-term strategy.

14. Next Steps

Following a community feedback period, the goal is to reach consensus on recognizing Maxshot as an official ecosystem partner. Maxshot remains committed to transparent engagement and ongoing communication with the Aave DAO.

15. Resources & Contacts

For questions, feedback, or collaboration inquiries, please reach out through the following channels:

  • Website: https://maxshot.ai/

  • Dapp: https://app.maxshot.ai/

  • X: https://twitter.com/MaxshotAI

  • Docs: https://docs.maxshot.ai

  • GitHub: https://github.com/dforce-network

  • BD & Marketing: contact@maxshot.ai

Hi everyone,

I currently have a fairly large leveraged position on Aave v4 Main Hub, with WBTC, wstETH, ETH and LINK as collateral.

After comparing the risk parameters, I’ve noticed that Bluechip Hub offers significantly higher collateral factors / liquidation thresholds for the assets that make up most of my collateral (particularly WBTC and wstETH). I’d like to migrate my existing position from Main Hub to Bluechip Hub, but I couldn’t find any documentation describing the process.

My questions are:

  1. Is there currently a supported way to migrate an existing position from Main Hub to Bluechip Hub?
  2. Is there an atomic (flash-loan) migration path, or does it require manually repaying debt, withdrawing collateral, redepositing into Bluechip, and borrowing again?
  3. Does Aave have any plans to support one-click migrations between hubs in the future?
  4. If no migration path exists today, what is the safest recommended approach for moving a large leveraged position while minimizing liquidation and market-exposure risk?

For context, this is a six-figure borrowing position, so I’m trying to avoid any unnecessary execution risk during the migration. I would rather not manually unwind the position as I don’t intend selling collateral assets at this time.

Any guidance from the Aave team or anyone who has done this would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

governance_posts.title: Risk Stewards: Supply and Borrow Cap Increases on Aave V3 / 2026.06.19
governance_posts.description:
Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following parameter changes based on user behavior, on-chain liquidity, and position health observed in the latest review of Aave V3 reserves.

Aave V3 Mantle:

  • Increase supply cap for sUSDe from 122,000,000 to 158,600,000.

Aave V3 X Layer:

  • Increase borrow cap for xSOL from 17,100 to 34,200.

sUSDe (Aave V3 Mantle)

sUSDe has reached 97.9% supply cap utilization (119,440,026 / 122,000,000) on the Aave V3 Mantle instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 19, 2026

The top sUSDe suppliers have a median health factor of 1.02 on V3 Mantle. Borrowing is concentrated in USDT0 at $119M (90.2% of total debt), followed by USDC and GHO, against sUSDe collateral, with most positions utilizing the Stablecoin E-Mode category (90% LTV, 92% LT).

Recommendation

We recommend raising the supply cap from 122,000,000 to 158,600,000 on Aave V3 Mantle. The reserve sits at 97.9% supply cap utilization, and the proposed cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 75.3% at current supply.

xSOL (Aave V3 X Layer)

xSOL has reached 100.0% borrow cap utilization (17,092 / 17,100) on the Aave V3 X Layer instance.

Borrow Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 19, 2026

The top xSOL borrowers have a median health factor of 1.44. Borrowing activity is predominantly concentrated in xSOL debt backed by USD₮0 collateral, with some positions utilizing a mix of other collateral assets whose oracle prices are not correlated with xSOL.

Recommendation

We recommend raising the borrow cap from 17,100 to 34,200 on Aave V3 X Layer. The reserve is fully utilized against its current borrow cap, and the proposed cap brings post-change borrow utilization to approximately 50% at current outstanding borrow.

Specification

Instance Asset Current Supply Cap Recommended Supply Cap Current Borrow Cap Recommended Borrow Cap
Aave V3 Mantle sUSDe 122,000,000 158,600,000 - —
Aave V3 X Layer xSOL 110,000 - 17,100 34,200

Next Steps

We will move forward and implement these updates via the Risk Steward process.

governance_posts.title: Risk Stewards: Supply and Borrow Cap Reductions on Aave V3 / 2026.06.30
governance_posts.description:
Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following parameter changes based on user behavior, on-chain liquidity, and position health observed in the latest review of Aave V3 reserves.

Aave V3 Sonic:

  • Reduce supply cap for USDC from 5,200,000 to 2,170,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for USDC from 4,600,000 to 1,050,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for WETH from 1,800 to 556.
  • Reduce borrow cap for WETH from 500 to 97.
  • Reduce supply cap for stS from 100,000,000 to 50,300,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for wS from 220,000,000 to 59,100,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for wS from 130,000,000 to 36,100,000.

Aave V3 Aptos:

  • Reduce supply cap for USDt from 4,500,000 to 1,040,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for USDt from 1,200,000 to 448,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for APT from 900,000 to 415,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for APT from 150,000 to 8,370.
  • Reduce supply cap for sUSDe from 500,000 to 2,600.
  • Reduce supply cap for USDC from 4,200,000 to 144,000.
  • Reduce borrow cap for USDC from 150,000 to 33,000.

Aave V3 Core:

  • Reduce supply cap for PT-srUSDe-25JUN2026 from 100,000,000 to 1.

Cap Reductions

These changes reduce the protocol’s maximum exposure to the Aave V3 Sonic and Aave V3 Aptos markets. Each cap is set to approximately 0.67x current outstanding, below present balances, so new supply and borrow are blocked while existing positions remain unaffected and can be unwound at the holder’s discretion. Current outstanding balances and cap utilization are shown below; the resulting caps appear in the Specification.

Instance Asset Supplied Supply util Borrowed Borrow util

Aave V3 Sonic stS 75,191,627 75.2% — —
Aave V3 Sonic wS 88,221,957 40.1% 53,943,713 41.5%
Aave V3 Aptos USDt 1,563,513 34.7% 669,892 55.8%
Aave V3 Aptos APT 619,797 68.9% 12,506 8.3%
Aave V3 Aptos sUSDe 3,885 0.8% — —
Aave V3 Aptos USDC 215,779 5.1% 49,384 32.9%

stS on Aave V3 Sonic and sUSDe on Aave V3 Aptos have borrowing disabled and are reduced on the supply side only. sUSDe and USDC on Aave V3 Aptos sit well below their current supply caps (0.8% and 5.1% utilization), so their proportional reductions are larger than on the other reserves.

PT-srUSDe-25JUN2026 on Aave V3 Core matured on 2026-06-25 and is redeemable at par against srUSDe. Its supply cap is set to 1, blocking new deposits while existing holders redeem at par; the reserve is collateral-only.

governance_posts.title: Risk Stewards: Supply and Borrow Cap Increases on Aave V3 / 2026.06.25
governance_posts.description:
Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following parameter changes based on user behavior, on-chain liquidity, and position health observed in the latest review of Aave V3 reserves.

Aave V3 Base:

  • Increase supply cap for cbETH from 29,900 to 39,900.

Aave V3 Avalanche:

  • Increase borrow cap for WAVAX from 10,055,500 to 11,700,000.

Aave V3 Core:

  • Increase supply cap for wstETH from 810,000 to 891,000.

  • Increase supply cap for USDe from 430,000,000 to 516,000,000.

  • Increase supply cap for syrupUSDT from 13,600,000 to 27,200,000.

cbETH (Aave V3 Base)

cbETH has reached 99.9% supply cap utilization (29,858 / 29,900) on the Aave V3 Base instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 24, 2026

The top twenty cbETH suppliers run health factors between 1.02 and 10.76 with a median of 1.14, and each carries outstanding debt. Most borrow WETH against cbETH collateral, an ETH-correlated pairing whose health factor tracks the spread between cbETH and ETH, which reduces liquidation risk.

Recommendation

We recommend raising the supply cap for cbETH from 29,900 to 39,900 on Aave V3 Base. cbETH sits at 99.9% supply cap utilization, and the new cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 74.8%.

WAVAX (Aave V3 Avalanche)

WAVAX has reached 99.9% borrow cap utilization (10,047,502 / 10,055,500) on the Aave V3 Avalanche instance.

Borrow Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 24, 2026

The top twenty WAVAX borrowers run health factors between 1.00 and 2.10 with a median of 1.04, and all carry WAVAX debt. Most post sAVAX as collateral, an AVAX-correlated position whose tight health-factor cluster tracks the spread between sAVAX and AVAX, with the exception of the main borrower in the market which is using BTC.b and WETH.e as collateral with a conservative HF.

Recommendation

We recommend raising the borrow cap for WAVAX from 10,055,500 to 11,700,000 on Aave V3 Avalanche. WAVAX sits at 99.9% borrow cap utilization, and the new cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 85.9%, with the supply cap held unchanged at 18,160,000.

wstETH (Aave V3 Core)

wstETH has reached 96.5% supply cap utilization (782,006 / 810,000) on the Aave V3 Core instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 24, 2026

The top twenty wstETH suppliers run health factors between 1.03 and 14.99 with a median of 1.50, with 19 of 20 carrying debt. The largest positions by value borrow WETH against wstETH, an ETH-correlated pairing whose health factor tracks the spread between wstETH and ETH, while a larger number borrow USDT and USDC, so the cohort mixes ETH-correlated and stablecoin borrowing against the LST.

Recommendation

We recommend raising the supply cap for wstETH from 810,000 to 891,000 on Aave V3 Core. wstETH sits at 96.5% supply cap utilization, and the new cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 87.8%.

USDe (Aave V3 Core)

USDe has reached 96.4% supply cap utilization (414,666,113 / 430,000,000) on the Aave V3 Core instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 24, 2026

The top twenty USDe suppliers run health factors between 1.02 and 7.17 with a median of 1.14, with 17 of 20 carrying debt. Most borrow USDT and USDC against USDe collateral in stablecoin E-Mode.

Recommendation

We recommend raising the supply cap for USDe from 430,000,000 to 516,000,000 on Aave V3 Core. USDe sits at 96.4% supply cap utilization, and the new cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 80.4%, with the borrow cap held unchanged at 290,000,000.

syrupUSDT (Aave V3 Core)

syrupUSDT is currently at 87.2% supply cap utilization (11,861,446 / 13,600,000) on the Aave V3 Core instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 24, 2026

The top syrupUSDT suppliers run health factors between 1.00 and 2.74 with a median of 1.11, and all carry outstanding debt. Most borrow USDT against syrupUSDT collateral in the syrupUSDT Stablecoins E-Mode.

Recommendation

We recommend doubling the supply cap for syrupUSDT from 13,600,000 to 27,200,000 on Aave V3 Core. syrupUSDT sits at 87.2% supply cap utilization, and the new cap brings post-change utilization to approximately 43.6%.

Summary

LlamaRisk recommends the following parameter changes based on user behavior, on-chain liquidity, and position health observed in the latest review of Aave V3 reserves.

Aave V3 Plasma:

  • Increase supply cap for syrupUSDT from 227,000,000 to 300,000,000.
  • Reduce supply cap for PT-USDe-18JUN2026 from 30,000,000 to 1.
  • Reduce supply cap for PT-sUSDE-18JUN2026 from 260,000,000 to 1.

syrupUSDT (Aave V3 Plasma)

syrupUSDT has reached 99.96% supply cap utilization (226,906,353 / 227,000,000) on the Aave V3 Plasma instance.

Supply Distribution


Source: LlamaRisk, June 22, 2026

The top twenty syrupUSDT suppliers run health factors between 1.02 and 1.08 with a median of 1.03, and all twenty carry outstanding debt. Fifteen borrow USDT0 and five borrow GHO against syrupUSDT collateral within the stablecoin E-Mode categories, so health factor stability depends on syrupUSDT holding its value against the borrowed stablecoins.

Recommendation

We recommend increasing the supply cap from 227,000,000 to 300,000,000 on Aave V3 Plasma. Supply of 226,906,353 sits at 99.96% of the existing cap; the proposed cap brings utilization to approximately 75.6% and restores headroom for continued deposit demand.

Cap Reductions

The following matured Pendle PTs are being wound down. After maturity each PT is redeemable at par against its underlying, and setting the supply cap to 1 blocks new deposits without affecting existing holders’ redemptions.

PT-USDe-18JUN2026 on Aave V3 Plasma matured on June 18, 2026 and sits at 0.0% supply cap utilization (146 / 30,000,000), with the supply cap reduced to 1.

PT-sUSDE-18JUN2026 on Aave V3 Plasma matured on June 18, 2026 and sits at 14.0% supply cap utilization (36,272,610 / 260,000,000), with the supply cap reduced to 1.
